item.title: Spending more money on homelessness isn’t helping
item.description: What are New York taxpayers getting for $81,705 a year per unsheltered homeless person?

item.title: Trump Struggles To Open The Strait of Hormuz
item.description: An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.

item.title: Terms of surrender in the war on wind
item.description: Courts help the U.S. move toward a truly all-the-above energy strategy.

item.title: Unraveling the FDA’s shortsighted ban on flavored vapes
item.description: The government’s crackdown on e-cigarettes created a more dangerous black market.

item.title: It’s time for U.S. boots on the ground in Ukraine
item.description: Putin, covid vaccine policy and tariffs, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: A prolonged war in Iran could hand China the commodity it prizes most
item.description: But a short, decisive campaign could cause Beijing to think twice about taking Taiwan.

item.title: A general’s lost journal reveals life inside the British siege of Boston
item.description: Breaking history: A key moment in the Revolution looks different through John Burgoyne’s eyes

item.title: When 120 ships carried Britain out of Boston Harbor
item.description: George Washington’s first great victory came nine months before Trenton.

item.title: Britain is ready to admit it has an America problem
item.description: Washington’s unquestioned leadership of the West’s postwar alliance system is dissolving.

item.title: For all but two nations, the AI race is already over
item.description: Middle powers such as Europe and Canada need to get smart about artificial intelligence.

item.title: When the Iran war ends, the mullahs will be broke
item.description: Even if the regime retains power, the country’s economic nightmare will persist.

item.title: RFK Jr. wants doctors to push healthy eating. Here’s what he forgot to ask.
item.description: There’s a reason nutrition isn’t already a bigger part of medical education.

item.title: Paul Ehrlich has died. His shock waves remain.
item.description: The dire predictions in “Population Bomb” are thoroughly discredited but still causing damage.

item.title: Moving the goalpost
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the Iran war outcomes and timeline.

item.title: How to solve the Hormuz crisis
item.description: Air supremacy is not the same as sea control. A better bet may be to declare victory and walk away.

item.title: Brendan Carr, minister of truth
item.description: The FCC chairman keeps showing why his agency shouldn’t exist.

item.title: The scientific consensus on daylight saving is bafflingly wrong
item.description: Sunlight, scalpers and symphonies, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: What’s capturing America’s attention? Sign up for Fanfare With Will Leitch.
item.description: Every Thursday, this newsletter will unpack the stories and moments we can’t stop thinking about.

item.title: America faces an education depression. Why are Democrats silent?
item.description: The party has focused on defending institutions instead of empowering families.

item.title: The Iran war is eroding America’s China deterrent
item.description: U.S. tactical gains are consuming the ships, munitions and readiness needed for the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: The U.S. military’s greatest weakness in Iran is one it can’t fix
item.description: Many issues can be resolved by smarter acquisitions decisions. Not so for incompetent leadership.

item.title: I’ve seen several types of warfare. This is the worst.
item.description: Drones are erasing any sense of safety, and Iran is only a warmup.

item.title: I traveled to Ukraine to teach sociology. It left me amazed.
item.description: A university in Kyiv is under siege — and running more than 12 hours a day.

item.title: A devastating train tragedy is clouding JD Vance’s judgment
item.description: The White House’s push for the Railway Safety Act flies in the face of its own advice.
